# Composing results

## Overview
`ems_compose()` retrieves and processes results from a solved model run. Results are parsed and filtered according to the `which` argument. Data validation and consistency checks are performed during the parsing process.

By default, [`ems_solve()`](solve.qmd) calls `ems_compose()` automatically, so this function is primarily used when `suppress_outputs = TRUE` was passed to [`ems_solve()`](solve.qmd) or [`solve_in_situ()`](solve.qmd), or when re-parsing results from a previous run.

## Arguments

| Argument | Default | Description |
|----------|---------|-------------|
| `cmf_path` | | Path to the CMF file generated by [`ems_deploy()`](deploy.qmd) |
| `which` | `"all"` | `"all"` returns all model variables and all coefficients (if coefficient output files are present). Any other value is treated as a character vector of variable and/or coefficient names to retrieve — an error is raised for any name not found |

## Basic usage

After a standard model run with suppressed outputs:
```r
ems_solve(
  cmf_path = cmf_path,
  solution_method = "Johansen",
  matrix_method = "LU",
  suppress_outputs = TRUE
)

# Parse all results
outputs <- ems_compose(cmf_path = cmf_path)
```

## Filtering output

Pass a character vector of names to `which` to retrieve a subset of results.
Variables and coefficients can be mixed freely in a single call:

```r
# All model variables and coefficients (default)
all_outputs <- ems_compose(cmf_path = cmf_path)

# Single variable by name
qfd <- ems_compose(cmf_path = cmf_path, which = "qfd")

# Single coefficient by name
SAVE <- ems_compose(cmf_path = cmf_path, which = "SAVE")

# Multiple names — variables and coefficients can be mixed
results <- ems_compose(cmf_path = cmf_path, which = c("qfd", "qfm", "SAVE"))
```

Note that some coefficient names differ from their associated HAR headers.
For example, the coefficient exposed as `"VTMFSD"` corresponds to the `VTWR` header.
Use the name as it appears in the model file, not the header.

## Return value

`ems_compose()` returns a tibble with one row per result and four columns:

| Column | Description |
|--------|-------------|
| `name` | Variable or coefficient name as it appears in the model file |
| `label` | Description string from the model file |
| `type` | `"variable"` or `"coefficient"` |
| `dat` | List-column of data.tables, one per row, containing the result values |
